agents.stance: Prioritizes open civic access with targeted safeguards.
Prioritizes institutional stability and risk controls.

screening.reason: Static local-LM priors differ on topic-relevant access-versus-security values.

transcript.speaker: A
transcript.text:
My view has not changed.
Strongest opponent point: Opponents argue that government surveillance infringes on individual privacy rights and can lead to a loss of civil liberties.
Counterargument: While privacy is important, national security threats require robust measures to protect citizens. Balancing privacy and security through transparent policies and oversight mechanisms can mitigate concerns while ensuring safety.
View change: My view has not changed.

transcript.speaker: B
transcript.text:
Strongest opponent point: Opponents argue that government surveillance can lead to a loss of civil liberties and trust in governmental institutions.
Counterargument: Effective oversight and accountability measures can prevent abuses and ensure that surveillance powers are used responsibly. This balance protects both individual rights and national security interests.
View change: My view has not changed.

transcript.speaker: A
transcript.text:
Strongest opponent point: Opponents argue that government surveillance can be misused for political repression and targeting of minority groups.
Counterargument: Robust legal frameworks and independent oversight bodies can prevent such misuse by ensuring that surveillance targets are legitimate and non-discriminatory.
View change: My view has not changed.

transcript.speaker: B
transcript.text:
Strongest opponent point: Opponents argue that government surveillance can be conducted without sufficient transparency, leading to potential abuse and lack of public trust.
Counterargument: Implementing clear guidelines and regular audits can enhance transparency and accountability, ensuring that surveillance activities are conducted openly and ethically.
View change: My view has not changed.
